info.name: VulnGuard API Tests
info.description:
Comprehensive API test collection for VulnGuard vulnerability tracking platform.

Covers 81 endpoints with positive and negative test cases, including advanced filters ($in, $regex, $or), cursor-based pagination, geospatial queries ($geoNear), token blacklisting, $out aggregation, and bulk insert_many operations.

**Seeded-data only** — starts from login (no registration). All user accounts come from the seed script.

Every request includes a Markdown description documenting purpose and expected outcome.

Module: COM661 – Full Stack Strategies and Development

exec: raw: JSON.stringify({ email: email, password: password })

request.description:
Authenticate as the admin user and store the JWT token in `admin_token` environment variable.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data.token`.

Authenticate as the analyst user and store the JWT token in `analyst_token`.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data.token`.

Authenticate as the guest user and store the JWT token in `guest_token`.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data.token`.

Authenticate with valid admin credentials.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with JWT token containing `user_id`, `username`, `role`, `exp`, `iat`, `jti`.

exec: pm.test('Login response has token and user', function () {

request.description:
Attempt login with an incorrect password.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Attempt login with an email that does not exist in the database.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Fetch the current user's profile using `x-access-token` header.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with user details.

Attempt to access profile without providing any authentication token.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Obtain a new JWT token before the current one expires.

**Method:** `POST /auth/refresh`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with new token.

Attempt password change with incorrect current password.

**Expects:** `400 Bad Request`.

Fetch user profile using the `x-access-token` header as the standard authentication header.

Uses the `x-access-token` header as specified by the coursework.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data.username`.

Invalidate the current JWT by adding it to the `blacklist` MongoDB collection.

**Method:** `POST /auth/logout`
**Expects:** `200 OK` — token is now blacklisted.

The blacklisted token is saved for the next test.

exec: // Save the now-blacklisted token for next test
// Force token refresh on next request

request.description:
Attempt to access a protected endpoint with a token that has been blacklisted via logout.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized` — "Token has been cancelled".

{
  "vulnerability_title": "SQL Injection in Login Form - Test",
  "description": "The login form is vulnerable to SQL injection through the username field, allowing authentication bypass.",
  "cve_id": "CVE-2026-99001",
  "severity": "Critical",
  "status": "Open",
  "cvss_score": 9.8,

  "patch_due_date": "2026-04-15T00:00:00Z",

  "reported_by": "security-scanner"
}

request.description:
Create a new vulnerability with all required fields (vulnerability_title, description, severity, status, cvss_score, asset_name, asset_type, vulnerability_type, discovery_method, department, reported_by).

**Method:** `POST /vulnerabilities/`
**Expects:** `201 Created` with the full document including `_id`.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 201 Created', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(201); });
pm.test('Vulnerability created with all fields', function () {

request.description:
Submit vulnerability creation with missing required fields.

**Expects:** `400 Bad Request` with detailed field-level error messages.

Submit a vulnerability with `cvss_score` outside the valid 0–10 range.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

Submit a vulnerability with an invalid `severity` value (not in Critical/High/Medium/Low/Informational).

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

Attempt vulnerability creation without any authentication token.

request.description:
Attempt vulnerability creation as a guest user (insufficient permissions).

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden` — RBAC rejects guest role.

Retrieve vulnerabilities using default pagination (skip/limit).

**Method:** `GET /vulnerabilities/`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data` array and `pagination` metadata.

item.name: Get All Vulnerabilities – Filter by Severity (200)

request.description:
Filter vulnerabilities using exact-match `severity` query parameter.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — all results match the specified severity.

item.name: Get All Vulnerabilities – Sort by CVSS Descending (200)

request.description:
Retrieve vulnerabilities sorted by `cvss_score` in descending order.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — results ordered highest CVSS first.

Use MongoDB `$text` search via the `search` query parameter.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — results matching the search terms.

Test skip/limit offset pagination by requesting `page=2`.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `pagination.page: 2`.

Fetch a single vulnerability by its ObjectId.

**Method:** `GET /vulnerabilities/:id`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with full document.

item.name: Get Single Vulnerability – Invalid ID Format (422)

request.description:
Request a vulnerability with a malformed ObjectId string.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity` — ObjectId validation.

Request a vulnerability with a valid but non-existent ObjectId.

**Expects:** `404 Not Found`.

Partial update of a vulnerability (e.g., change severity or status).

**Method:** `PUT /vulnerabilities/:id`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with updated document.

Attempt update with invalid `cvss_score`.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

Attempt deletion as analyst user. Only admins can delete.

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden` — RBAC enforcement.

Filter using MongoDB `$in` operator via `severity_in=Critical,High` query parameter.

Return only vulnerabilities whose severity is one of the comma-separated values.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — all results have severity Critical or High.

exec: pm.test('All results are Critical or High', function () {

request.description:
Filter using MongoDB `$in` operator via `status_in=Open,In Progress` query parameter.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — all results have matching status.

exec: pm.test('All results match status filter', function () {

request.description:
Filter using MongoDB `$regex` operator via `title_regex=SQL.*Injection` query parameter.

Case-insensitive regex matching on the `vulnerability_title` field.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — all titles match the pattern.

Retrieve first page using cursor-based (keyset) pagination with `sort_order=desc`.

Saves the last `_id` for the next-page test.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `pagination` metadata.

exec: // Save last id for next page test

request.description:
Fetch the next page of results using the `after` parameter (keyset pagination).

Uses the `_id` saved from the previous request. No duplicate documents across pages.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `pagination.next_after`.

Find vulnerabilities near Belfast (lat 54.60, lng -5.93) using the `$geoNear` aggregation stage.

Requires a `2dsphere` index on `vulnerabilities.location`.

**Expects:** `200 OK` — results include `distance_km` field.

Call the `/nearby` endpoint without the required `lat` parameter.

**Expects:** `400 Bad Request` — parameter validation.

Create multiple vulnerabilities in a single request using MongoDB `insert_many`.

Accepts a `vulnerabilities` array (2–50 items). Each item is validated individually; if any fail the entire batch is rejected.

**Method:** `POST /vulnerabilities/bulk`
**Expects:** `201 Created` with all created documents.

body.raw:
{
  "vulnerabilities": [
    {
      "vulnerability_title": "Bulk Test Vuln Alpha",
      "description": "First bulk-created vulnerability for testing insert_many.",
      "severity": "High",
      "status": "Open",
      "cvss_score": 8.1,
      "asset_name": "bulk-server-01",
      "asset_type": "Server",
      "vulnerability_type": "Software",
      "discovery_method": "Scan",
      "department": "Engineering",
      "reported_by": "bulk-test"
    },
    {
      "vulnerability_title": "Bulk Test Vuln Beta",
      "description": "Second bulk-created vulnerability for testing insert_many.",
      "severity": "Medium",
      "status": "Open",
      "cvss_score": 5.5,
      "asset_name": "bulk-server-02",
      "asset_type": "Server",
      "vulnerability_type": "Software",
      "discovery_method": "Scan",
      "department": "Engineering",
      "reported_by": "bulk-test"
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Attempt bulk creation with only 1 item (minimum is 2).

**Expects:** `400 Bad Request`.

Attempt bulk creation where one item passes validation but another fails.

The entire batch is rejected.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity` with per-item errors.

Attempt bulk creation as guest user.

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden` — RBAC enforcement.

Filter using MongoDB `$or` operator: returns vulnerabilities where severity=Critical **OR** status=Open.

Demonstrates combined `$or` query construction.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with matching results.

body.raw:
{
  "step_number": 1,
  "step_description": "Apply parameterised queries to all database interactions in the login module",
  "recommended_by": "j.smith",
  "status": "Pending",

request.description:
Add a remediation step sub-document using `$push` to the vulnerability's `remediation_steps` array.

**Method:** `POST /vulnerabilities/:id/remediation-steps`
**Expects:** `201 Created`.

Attempt to add a remediation step with missing required fields.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

Attempt to add a remediation step without authentication.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Retrieve all remediation steps for a vulnerability.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data` array of sub-documents.

Retrieve a single remediation step using positional `$` operator.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with matching sub-document.

Update a remediation step sub-document using positional `$` update.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Mark a remediation step as completed by updating its `status` field.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Remove a remediation step sub-document using `$pull`.

**Method:** `DELETE /vulnerabilities/:id/remediation-steps/:sid`
**Expects:** `204 No Content`.

Add an activity log sub-document to a vulnerability using `$push`.

**Method:** `POST /vulnerabilities/:id/activity-log`
**Expects:** `201 Created`.

item.name: Add Activity Log Entry – Missing Fields (422)

request.description:
Attempt to add an activity log entry with missing required fields.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

item.name: Add Activity Log Entry – No Auth (401)

request.description:
Attempt to add an activity log entry without authentication.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Retrieve all activity log entries for a vulnerability.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with `data` array.

Retrieve a single activity log entry from the sub-document array.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

item.name: Delete Activity Log Entry – Analyst Forbidden (403)

request.description:
Attempt log entry deletion as analyst. Only admins can delete.

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden`.

Delete an activity log entry as admin using `$pull`.

**Expects:** `204 No Content`.

Aggregation pipeline: `$group` by severity, `$project` count and percentage, `$sort`.

Returns the distribution of vulnerabilities across severity levels.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Aggregation pipeline: `$group` by department with `$avg` cvss_score, `$sum` count.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with per-department risk data.

Aggregation pipeline: `$match` past deadline and not patched, `$project` overdue info.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Aggregation pipeline: `$facet` with patched vs total counts, `$project` compliance percentage.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with compliance rate.

Aggregation pipeline: `$group` by date (`$dateToString`), `$sort` chronological.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with time-series trend data.

Aggregation pipeline: `$group` by asset_name, `$sort` by count descending.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with ranked asset list.

Aggregation pipeline: `$match` resolved items, `$group` with `$avg` using `$dateDiff`.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with mean remediation time.

Aggregation pipeline: `$match`, `$project` risk_score, `$sort` descending.

Requires admin or analyst role.

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Attempt to access risk scores as a guest user.

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden` — RBAC enforcement.

Aggregation pipeline: `$facet` combining multiple analytics into a single summary response.

**Expects:** `200 OK` with dashboard data.

Attempt to access an analytics endpoint without authentication.

**Expects:** `401 Unauthorized`.

Aggregation pipeline with `$out` stage: `$group` by department+severity, `$project`, `$sort`, `$out` to `reports` collection.

Writes aggregated results to a persistent MongoDB collection.

**Method:** `POST /analytics/generate-report`
**Expects:** `201 Created` with generated rows.

exec: pm.test('Row has department and severity', function () {

request.description:
Read documents from the `reports` collection that were written by the `$out` aggregation stage.

**Method:** `GET /analytics/reports`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with report data.

List all registered users (admin only).

**Method:** `GET /admin/users`
**Expects:** `200 OK` with array of users.

Also extracts the guest user's `_id` and stores it as `test_user_id` for subsequent admin management tests.

exec: // Extract guest user ID for subsequent admin tests
pm.test('Extract guest user ID for admin tests', function () {

request.description:
Attempt to list users as a non-admin user.

**Expects:** `403 Forbidden`.

Fetch a single user's details by ID (admin only).

**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Update a user's role (admin only). Changes guest user role to `analyst`.

**Method:** `PUT /admin/users/:id/role`
**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Post-test script automatically reverts the role back to `guest` to preserve seeded data.

exec: // Revert guest user role back to 'guest'
console.log('Reverted guest role back to guest');
console.error('Failed to revert guest role:', err || res.text());

request.description:
Attempt to assign an invalid role value.

**Expects:** `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

Deactivate a user account (admin only). Targets the guest user.

**Method:** `PUT /admin/users/:id/status`
**Expects:** `200 OK`.

Post-test script immediately reactivates the guest user to preserve seeded data integrity.

exec: // Reactivate guest user immediately to preserve seeded data
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ is_active: true }) }
console.error('Failed to reactivate guest:', err || res.text());

request.description:
Attempt to delete a user as a non-admin (analyst). Should be forbidden.

**Method:** `DELETE /admin/users/:id`
**Expects:** `403 Forbidden`. No data is modified.

Delete a user account (admin only).

**Pre-request:** Creates a disposable user via `POST /auth/register` so that seeded users are never deleted.

**Method:** `DELETE /admin/users/:id`
**Expects:** `204 No Content`.

exec: // Create a disposable user for the delete test (preserves seeded data)
console.error('Failed to create disposable user:', err || res.text());

request.description:
Clean up the test vulnerability created during the test run.

**Expects:** `204 No Content` or `404 Not Found` (already cleaned).

Clean up the first vulnerability created by the bulk-create test.

**Method:** `DELETE /vulnerabilities/:id`
**Expects:** `204 No Content`.

exec: // Skip the first one (that's handled by this request itself)
